reasoning: Sudan has been embroiled in a severe, devastating civil war since April 2023, severely compromising state capacity and legislative functions. As of April 2025, Sudan was explicitly listed as having signed but not yet ratified the AfCFTA agreement, being one of only a handful of remaining non-ratifying African countries. Given that the forecast date is June 6, 2025—leaving less than a month until the July 1, 2025 resolution deadline—and there are no reports of any imminent legislative action by the Sudanese government to ratify the agreement amid the existential conflict, the likelihood of ratification occurring in the next three weeks is extraordinarily low.
